member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Bounds2D
member.summary: An axis-aligned bounding box in the 2D XZ plane. Used for room-local outline extents, world-space floor-plan extents, and quick adjacency screening.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Bounds2D.Contains(System.Numerics.Vector2)
member.summary: True if is within these bounds (inclusive).

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Bounds2D.Union(OpenApparatus.Bounds2D,OpenApparatus.Bounds2D)
member.summary: Smallest bounds containing both inputs.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Bounds2D.Encapsulate(System.Numerics.Vector2)
member.summary: Smallest bounds containing the original plus .

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.EdgeSegment
member.summary:
A directed line segment in the 2D XZ plane. Used as the unit of room-outline description and inter-room adjacency: one edge of a room outline, or the shared boundary between two rooms.
Direction matters: walking from to , the "inside" of the room or adjacency is on the left (CCW outline convention).

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.EdgeSegment.Direction
member.summary: Unit vector from Start toward End. Undefined for zero-length segments.

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.EdgeSegment.Normal
member.summary: Unit vector 90° counter-clockwise from . For an outline edge ordered CCW around the room, this normal points OUTWARD (away from the room's interior). For an adjacency segment with RoomA on the left, it points toward RoomB.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.EdgeSegment.Reversed
member.summary: Returns the same segment with Start and End swapped (and Direction/Normal flipped).

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Geometry.AssembledRoomMesh
member.summary: One room's combined mesh as produced by : the original alongside its assembled (floor + ceiling + assigned walls, one per submesh).

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Geometry.AssembledRoomMesh.#ctor(OpenApparatus.Topology.Room,OpenApparatus.Geometry.MeshData)
member.summary: One room's combined mesh as produced by : the original alongside its assembled (floor + ceiling + assigned walls, one per submesh).

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Geometry.BoundaryWallBuilder
member.summary:
Builds wall geometry for one . The wall is a thick slab centered on the adjacency's , split equally on each side of the segment, running from y=0 to the requested height.
Output: • → 6-face closed wall slab in submesh . • → empty MeshData (no geometry). • → wall slab with rectangular tunnel through the full thickness.
All output is written to submesh ; the returned mesh has submeshes (Floor and Ceiling submeshes are present but empty), so it can be combined with per-room interior MeshData in the assembler.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Geometry.BoundaryWallBuilder.EmitClosedWallFaces(OpenApparatus.Geometry.MeshDataBuilder,OpenApparatus.Geometry.BoundaryWallBuilder.SlabFrame,System.Single)
member.summary: Emits the 6 faces of a fully-closed wall slab.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Geometry.BoundaryWallBuilder.EmitFullHeightSection(OpenApparatus.Geometry.MeshDataBuilder,OpenApparatus.Geometry.BoundaryWallBuilder.SlabFrame,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Emits the CellA + CellB face of a full-height wall section [xStart, xEnd] × [0, h].

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Geometry.BoundaryWallBuilder.EmitLintel(OpenApparatus.Geometry.MeshDataBuilder,OpenApparatus.Geometry.BoundaryWallBuilder.SlabFrame,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Emits CellA + CellB faces for a lintel — wall section [xStart, xEnd] × [yBot, yTop].

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Geometry.BoundaryWallBuilder.EmitBottomStrip(OpenApparatus.Geometry.MeshDataBuilder,OpenApparatus.Geometry.BoundaryWallBuilder.SlabFrame,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Emits the bottom face strip [xStart, xEnd] at y=0 (normal -Y).

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Geometry.BoundaryWallBuilder.SlabFrame
member.summary: A coordinate frame for a wall slab: an origin (segment start), a "direction" axis along the segment, a "normal" axis perpendicular to it (in XZ, +N = RoomA side), and a length. turns slab-local (along, height, side) into world XYZ.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Geometry.BoundaryWallBuilder.SlabFrame.Corner(System.Boolean,System.Single,System.Single)
T:OpenApparatus.Geometry.IShapeGeometryBuilder
member.summary:
Strategy that builds a thick-walled room mesh from a shape. One implementation per kind (e.g. ).
The result is a with three submeshes (, , ).
In v1 the builder produces a fully-closed room — every wall present, no doorways. Doorways and shared-wall ownership are introduced in milestone A3 and onward.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Geometry.IShapeGeometryBuilder.Build(OpenApparatus.Topology.Room,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Build the geometry for one room.
member.param: The room to mesh. Its must match the implementation's expected shape type.
Wall thickness, taken inward from the room's footprint (so the visible interior is (W − 2t) × (D − 2t) for a rectangle).
Height of the wall in world units. Floor at y=0, ceiling at y=wallHeight.

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Geometry.MeshData
member.summary:
Engine-agnostic mesh representation. Consumers (Unity adapter, Avalonia studio's OBJ export, etc.) convert this into their respective renderable type.
Vertex attributes are parallel arrays (vertex[i] has normal[i] and uv0[i]). Submeshes are slices of one shared vertex/normal/UV array, each with their own triangle index list — this matches the conventions of Unity's Mesh, Three.js BufferGeometry, glTF primitives, and most other modern engines.
Triangle winding: CCW when viewed from the side the normal points to.

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.Geometry.MeshData.SubmeshIndices
member.summary: One triangle index list per submesh. Indices reference the global vertex array.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Geometry.MeshData.Combine(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{OpenApparatus.Geometry.MeshData})
member.summary: Concatenates several mesh-datas into one. Vertex/normal/UV arrays are appended; submesh triangles are merged by index, with indices offset by the running vertex count so each input's triangles continue to reference its own vertices. Submesh count of the result = max submesh count across inputs.

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Geometry.MeshDataBuilder
member.summary:
Incremental builder for . Owns growing vertex/normal/UV lists plus per-submesh triangle index lists.
Vertices are appended per face (no sharing across faces — keeps normals sharp at edges). Submesh indices are looked up by integer key; missing keys auto-create.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Geometry.MeshDataBuilder.AddQuad(System.Int32,System.Numerics.Vector3,System.Numerics.Vector3,System.Numerics.Vector3,System.Numerics.Vector3,System.Numerics.Vector2,System.Numerics.Vector2,System.Numerics.Vector2,System.Numerics.Vector2)
member.summary: Adds a quad face with corners , , , in CCW order viewed from the side the normal points to. UVs are taken at the same indices.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Geometry.MeshDataBuilder.AddQuadAutoUv(System.Int32,System.Numerics.Vector3,System.Numerics.Vector3,System.Numerics.Vector3,System.Numerics.Vector3)
member.summary: Adds a quad face whose UVs are auto-generated from face-local distance: uvA=(0,0), uvB=(width,0), uvC=(width,height), uvD=(0,height) where width = |b-a| and height = |d-a|.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Geometry.MeshDataBuilder.EnsureSubmeshCount(System.Int32)
member.summary: Forces the output to have at least submeshes, padding any unused indices with empty arrays. Useful when the builder hasn't touched all conventional submeshes but downstream code expects them.

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Geometry.MultiRoomEnvironmentMeshAssembler
member.summary:
Walks a and produces one merged per room. The result is a list of in the same order as .
Per room, the assembler: 1. Builds the room's interior (floor + ceiling) via the appropriate I*InteriorBuilder for the room's shape. 2. For each adjacency the room touches, decides whether this room owns the wall (lower-id ownership for internal adjacencies; RoomA owns its outer adjacencies). Walls owned by this room are built via and appended to its mesh parts. 3. Combines all parts with .
In v1 only is supported. Other shapes will throw — extending support means adding a builder for that shape and a dispatch case below.
Known limitation: at outer corners of a building, walls don't extend past their segment ends, so a t/2 × t/2 square gap remains. To be fixed in a subsequent milestone (corner posts, or wall length extension).

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Geometry.MultiRoomEnvironmentMeshAssembler.ResolveWallOwner(OpenApparatus.Topology.Adjacency)
member.summary: The room whose mesh will contain the wall geometry for this adjacency. • Outer adjacency: RoomA (the only room). • Internal adjacency: lower-id room.

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Geometry.RectangleGeometryBuilder
member.summary:
Builds a for a single fully-closed room whose shape is a . The room becomes a thick-walled box with three submeshes (floor, walls, ceiling).
Faces emitted (18 total): • 1 floor quad (interior, normal +Y) • 1 ceiling quad (interior, normal −Y) • 4 outer wall quads — at the room footprint, normals point outward • 4 inner wall quads — at the inset interior boundary, normals point inward • 4 top frame quads — annular ring at y = wallHeight between outer and inner • 4 bottom frame quads — annular ring at y = 0 between outer and inner
Each face has its own 4 vertices (no sharing between faces) so normals stay sharp. Room rotation is supported.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Geometry.RectangleGeometryBuilder.AddVerticalWall(OpenApparatus.Geometry.MeshDataBuilder,OpenApparatus.Topology.Room,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Emit a vertical wall quad spanning from (ax, az) to (bx, bz) at the floor, extruded upward to . The face's outward normal is determined by the start→end direction (right-hand rule around +Y).

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Geometry.RectangleGeometryBuilder.AddHorizontalQuad(OpenApparatus.Geometry.MeshDataBuilder,OpenApparatus.Topology.Room,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Emit a horizontal quad in the XZ plane, axis-aligned, spanning [xMin..xMax] × [zMin..zMax] at the given y. =true → normal +Y; false → normal −Y.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Geometry.RectangleGeometryBuilder.World(OpenApparatus.Topology.Room,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Transform a room-local point (cellX in 2D X, cellZ in 2D Z, plus a world Y) into a world-space Vector3, applying the room's position and rotation.

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Geometry.RectangleInteriorBuilder
member.summary:
Builds the floor + ceiling of a rectangle room for the production pipeline. Uses the "split convention": walls live AT the room outline (centered with thickness t/2 on each side), so the interior occupies (t/2, t/2)..(W − t/2, D − t/2). The walls themselves are added separately by ; the assembler combines the two.
This is the production-side counterpart to the all-walls-included (kept for isolated-room preview).

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Geometry.RectangleInteriorBuilder.Build(OpenApparatus.Topology.Room,System.Single,System.Single)
T:OpenApparatus.Geometry.SubmeshIndex
member.summary: Conventional submesh indices used by every room-geometry builder. Engine adapters look these up to assign per-element materials (one floor material, one wall material, one ceiling material per room mesh).

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.SeededRandom
member.summary: Deterministic random number source. The single RNG used throughout floor-plan generation: every generator and passage assigner takes a , never a raw . This keeps "same seed → same plan" inviolable, which is the single most important reproducibility guarantee the library makes.

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.SeededRandom.Seed
member.summary: The seed that was used to construct this instance.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.SeededRandom.NextFloat
member.summary: Uniform float in [0, 1).

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.SeededRandom.NextInt(System.Int32)
member.summary: Uniform integer in [0, maxExclusive).

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.SeededRandom.NextInt(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Uniform integer in [minInclusive, maxExclusive).

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.SeededRandom.NextBool
member.summary: Fair coin flip.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.SeededRandom.Pick``1(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{``0})
member.summary: Returns a uniformly-chosen element from .

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.SeededRandom.Shuffle``1(System.Collections.Generic.IList{``0})
member.summary: In-place Fisher–Yates shuffle.

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Topology.Adjacency
member.summary: A boundary segment between two rooms, or between one room and the outside. Adjacencies are produced by an (always with initially) and then assigned passages by an to form the floor plan's connectivity.

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.Topology.Adjacency.RoomB
member.summary: Null when this is an outer-boundary adjacency (room ↔ outside).

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.Topology.Adjacency.SharedSegment
member.summary: The boundary segment in world XZ. Direction follows the CCW outline convention: walking Start → End, RoomA is on the left and RoomB (or outside) is on the right.

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.Topology.Adjacency.Passage
member.summary: Mutable — set by the passage assigner. Defaults to .

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.Topology.Adjacency.IsInternal
member.summary: True if this connects two rooms (false for outer-boundary adjacencies).

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.Topology.Adjacency.IsOuter
member.summary: True if this connects one room to the outside (false for inter-room adjacencies).

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Topology.Adjacency.Other(OpenApparatus.Topology.Room)
member.summary: Returns the other room across this adjacency, or null if outer.

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Topology.Assigners.SpanningTreePassageAssigner
member.summary:
Walks the floor plan's room adjacency graph and selects a random spanning tree — the cheapest data structure that guarantees exactly one path between any two rooms. Selected internal adjacencies become s; the rest stay . Optionally, one outer adjacency becomes a doorway too, representing the entrance from outside the floor.
Algorithm: Kruskal-with-shuffled-edges. Conceptually equivalent to assigning each edge a random weight and picking the minimum spanning tree, but cheaper.

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.Topology.Assigners.SpanningTreePassageAssigner.IncludeOuterEntrance
member.summary: If true, designates one outer adjacency as the floor's entrance doorway.

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.Topology.Assigners.SpanningTreePassageAssigner.PreferEntranceRoomType
member.summary: If non-null and is true, the outer entrance is preferentially placed on a leaf room whose matches. Falls back to any leaf if no matching room is on the boundary.

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Topology.Assigners.SpanningTreePassageAssigner.UnionFind
member.summary: Disjoint-set with union-by-rank + path compression. Internal helper.

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Topology.Generators.GridDominoGenerator
member.summary:
Tiles an axis-aligned floorWidth × floorLength grid of square tiles (each on a side) with: 1. randomly-placed 1×2 dominoes (rectangle rooms), 2. then 1×1 squares filling every remaining tile.
The output is a whose adjacencies are all initially ; pass it to an to open doors.

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.Topology.Generators.GridDominoGenerator.FloorWidthCells
member.summary: Number of tiles along the +X axis.

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.Topology.Generators.GridDominoGenerator.FloorLengthCells
member.summary: Number of tiles along the +Z axis.

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.Topology.Generators.GridDominoGenerator.RectangleRoomCount
member.summary: How many 1×2 rectangle rooms to place before filling with squares.

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.Topology.Generators.GridDominoGenerator.TileSize
member.summary: Side length of one grid tile (and therefore one square room) in world units.

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.Topology.Generators.GridDominoGenerator.MaxPlacementRetries
member.summary: Cap on the random-retry loop when a domino placement collides.

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.Topology.Generators.GridDominoGenerator.Orientation
member.summary: Controls whether each rectangle is laid length-wise (1×2 along +Z), width-wise (2×1 along +X), or randomly per-rectangle (the default).

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Topology.Generators.GridDominoGenerator.BuildAdjacencies(System.Int32[0:,0:],OpenApparatus.Topology.Room[])
member.summary: Walks the grid and emits one adjacency per directed boundary segment between distinct rooms (or between a room and the outside). Multiple grid-room-pairs between the same two rooms are collapsed into one adjacency with the merged world segment.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Topology.Generators.GridDominoGenerator.GridEdgeSegment(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the world-space edge segment of grid room (x, z) on the side indicated by (dx, dz). The direction is CCW around (x, z).

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Topology.Generators.GridDominoGenerator.MergeColinearSegments(System.Collections.Generic.List{OpenApparatus.EdgeSegment})
member.summary: Merges segments that share an endpoint AND a direction into single longer ones. All input segments in any one call are guaranteed colinear by the binning logic above.

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Topology.Generators.RectangleOrientation
member.summary: Controls how rectangle (1×2) rooms are oriented when placed by .

member.name: F:OpenApparatus.Topology.Generators.RectangleOrientation.Random
member.summary: Each rectangle's orientation is picked at random per placement.

member.name: F:OpenApparatus.Topology.Generators.RectangleOrientation.LengthWise
member.summary: All rectangles span along the floor's length axis (+Z) — i.e. each rectangle occupies a 1-tile-wide × 2-tile-long footprint.

member.name: F:OpenApparatus.Topology.Generators.RectangleOrientation.WidthWise
member.summary: All rectangles span along the floor's width axis (+X) — i.e. each rectangle occupies a 2-tile-wide × 1-tile-long footprint.

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Topology.IMultiRoomEnvironmentGenerator
member.summary: Strategy that produces a fresh from a seeded RNG. Implementations must be deterministic — same instance + same seed must always yield the same plan.

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Topology.IPassageAssigner
member.summary: Strategy that mutates the passages on a 's adjacencies (e.g. picking which closed walls become doorways) to define the floor's connectivity pattern.

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Topology.IRoomShape
member.summary: The 2D footprint of a room in room-local coordinates (XZ plane). Implementations expose the boundary as an ordered list of s, CCW from above, starting at a deterministic vertex (so two equal shapes always have the same edge ordering — this is required for golden-master tests).

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Topology.IRoomShape.GetOutline
member.summary: The shape's outline as a closed polyline in room-local coordinates, CCW from above (the room interior is on the left of each edge). Ordering is shape-specific but deterministic.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Topology.IRoomShape.GetLocalBounds
member.summary: Axis-aligned bounding box of the outline in room-local coordinates.

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Topology.MultiRoomEnvironment
member.summary: The output of : a set of rooms and the adjacencies between them (and to the outside). This is the engine-agnostic description of a floor plan; the geometry layer turns it into meshes.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Topology.MultiRoomEnvironment.GetWorldBounds
member.summary: Bounds in world coordinates encompassing every room.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Topology.MultiRoomEnvironment.GetAdjacenciesOf(OpenApparatus.Topology.Room)
member.summary: All adjacencies that touch (internal or outer).

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Topology.MultiRoomEnvironment.GetInternalAdjacencies
member.summary: All adjacencies between two rooms (excludes outer boundaries).

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Topology.MultiRoomEnvironment.GetOuterAdjacencies
member.summary: All adjacencies between a room and the outside.

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Topology.MultiRoomEnvironmentBuilder
member.summary:
Constructs a from an authored tile grid. This is the editor-driven counterpart to : the user lays out which tiles belong to which room, the builder turns that grid into the materialized environment with adjacencies derived from it.
Grid convention: • grid[x, z] == -1 → empty tile (no room there; counts as "outside") • grid[x, z] >= 0 → that tile belongs to room with that id
In v1, every room must occupy a contiguous, axis-aligned rectangular set of tiles. Non-rectangular tile sets throw — polygon-shaped rooms come later.
All adjacencies in the returned environment are ; pass it to an or mutate the passages in the editor to define connectivity.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Topology.MultiRoomEnvironmentBuilder.BuildAdjacencies(System.Int32[0:,0:],System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.Int32,OpenApparatus.Topology.Room},System.Single)
member.summary: Walks the grid and produces one adjacency per inter-room shared boundary segment (for distinct rooms) or per outer-boundary segment (for empty / out-of-bounds neighbors). Each internal boundary is visited exactly once. Outer boundaries are visited per side.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Topology.MultiRoomEnvironmentBuilder.GridEdgeSegment(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Single)
member.summary: Returns the world-space edge segment of grid tile (x, z) on the side indicated by (dx, dz). Direction is CCW around the tile.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Topology.MultiRoomEnvironmentBuilder.MergeColinearSegments(System.Collections.Generic.List{OpenApparatus.EdgeSegment})
member.summary: Merge segments that share an endpoint AND a direction into one longer segment.

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Topology.Passage
member.summary: What lies along the boundary an represents. Sealed hierarchy — extend by adding a new nested type, never by inheriting outside this file.

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Topology.Passage.Closed
member.summary: A solid wall — no opening between the two rooms (or to the outside).

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Topology.Passage.Open
member.summary: No wall at all. The two rooms share floor space (e.g. T-maze internal arms).

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Topology.Passage.Doorway
member.summary: A solid wall with one or more rectangular openings cut into it. Each opening sits on the floor (y=0) and rises to the opening's height.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Topology.Passage.Doorway.#ctor(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Convenience constructor for the common single-opening case.

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Topology.Opening
member.summary:
One rectangular opening in a doorway wall. is the distance from the shared segment's Start to the opening's left edge. is the bottom of the opening; 0 = a door (sits on the floor), >0 = a window (a wall panel below the opening). is the top of the opening (head height), measured from the floor.
and are presentation hints for door panels: they pick which end of the opening the hinge sits on and which side of the wall the door swings into. The geometric opening (the rectangular hole through the wall) is unaffected — these flags are read by 2D editors and render-time door props in downstream tools.

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.Topology.Opening.IsWindow
member.summary: True when > 0 — the opening floats above the floor.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Topology.Opening.With(System.Nullable{System.Single},System.Nullable{System.Single},System.Nullable{System.Single},System.Nullable{System.Single},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Nullable{System.Boolean})
member.summary: Returns a copy with the named field replaced. Lets editors mutate one piece of state without rebuilding the whole struct in client code.

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Topology.RectangleShape
member.summary:
A width × depth axis-aligned rectangle, with its local origin at the south-west (min) corner and edges in the order [South, East, North, West].
Coordinate convention: +X = east, +Z = north (in 2D, +Y component of the stored Vector2 represents the +Z world axis).

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Topology.RectangleShape.GetOutline
member.summary: Outline ordered CCW from above: [0] South: (0,0) → (W,0) [1] East: (W,0) → (W,D) [2] North: (W,D) → (0,D) [3] West: (0,D) → (0,0)

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Topology.Room
member.summary:
A single room / region of a floor plan. Owns a outline, a world-space placement, and a classification used by generators.
The shape lives in room-local coordinates with the local origin defined by the shape implementation. World-space outline is computed by translating + rotating the local outline by and .

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.Topology.Room.Position
member.summary: World XZ position of the room's local-origin point.

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.Topology.Room.Rotation
member.summary: Rotation about the +Y axis, in degrees. CCW positive (looking down).

member.name: P:OpenApparatus.Topology.Room.RoomType
member.summary: Generator-assigned room type. Drives downstream behavior (e.g. entrance preference).

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Topology.Room.GetWorldOutline
member.summary: The room's outline transformed into world coordinates.

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Topology.Room.LocalToWorld(System.Numerics.Vector2,System.Single)
member.summary: Transform a room-local 2D point (in XZ) into a world-space 3D point at the given , applying and .

member.name: M:OpenApparatus.Topology.Room.GetWorldBounds
member.summary: The room's bounds in world coordinates (axis-aligned even if shape is rotated).

member.name: T:OpenApparatus.Topology.RoomType
member.summary: Classification of a room placed by a tiling generator. Currently used by GridDominoGenerator to distinguish single-tile rooms from 1×2 dominoes.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsExternalInit
member.summary: Reserved by the compiler for record types and `init` setters. The marker is shipped in .NET 5+; we polyfill it here so we can use those features while still multi-targeting netstandard2.1 for Unity compatibility.
